119-hjres-20.one_liner: Cancel a Department of Energy rule setting new efficiency standards for gas water heaters
provisions: Cancels a Department of Energy rule that set new efficiency standards for gas-fired instant water heaters
Blocks efficiency requirements that the Energy Department said would achieve maximum energy savings while being technically possible and economically justified
Prevents the new standards from taking effect after the rule was submitted in December 2024
Stops efficiency rules that would have applied to gas water heaters used in homes and businesses

119-hjres-24.one_liner: Cancel Department of Energy rule setting new energy standards for walk-in coolers and freezers
provisions: Cancels a Department of Energy rule made on December 23, 2024
The rule set new energy conservation standards for walk-in coolers and walk-in freezers
Businesses that make or use walk-in coolers and freezers will not have to follow the new standards
The energy standards were meant to make the equipment use less electricity

119-hjres-25.one_liner: Cancel an IRS rule requiring brokers to report cryptocurrency sales to the government
provisions: Cancels an IRS rule that started December 30, 2024
The rule required brokers who help with cryptocurrency transactions to report sales information to the IRS
This applies to decentralized finance transactions involving digital assets like Bitcoin
Brokers would no longer need to report this information if the bill passes

119-hjres-35.one_liner: Overturn an EPA rule that charges oil and gas companies fees for methane emissions
provisions: Overturns an EPA rule published on November 18, 2024
Stops the EPA from collecting annual fees from oil and gas companies when their methane emissions exceed set waste limits
Removes rules that let companies pay lower fees by offsetting emissions between facilities or qualifying for exemptions
Ends the Methane Emissions Reduction Program's fee collection system

119-hjres-42.one_liner: Cancel a Department of Energy rule changing appliance energy reporting and labeling
provisions: Cancels a Department of Energy rule published on October 9, 2024
The rule changed reporting rules for appliances like washing machines and dishwashers
The rule changed labeling rules for consumer products and industrial equipment
The rule changed enforcement rules for energy efficiency standards
The rule was meant to align reporting with existing energy efficiency rules
The rule was meant to help DOE decide which energy rules apply to which products

119-hjres-60.one_liner: Cancel a National Park Service rule limiting off-road vehicles in Glen Canyon
provisions: Cancels a National Park Service rule issued on January 13, 2025
The rule limited off-road vehicles in Glen Canyon National Recreation Area
The area is in northern Arizona and southeastern Utah
Off-road vehicles would continue under the previous rules

119-hjres-61.one_liner: Cancel an EPA rule limiting air pollution from tire factories
provisions: Cancels an EPA rule about air pollution from tire factories
The EPA rule set limits on air pollution from tire manufacturing
The rule was published in November 2024
The rule required tire factories to control air emissions

119-hjres-75.one_liner: Cancel a Department of Energy rule on commercial refrigerator energy standards
provisions: Cancels a Department of Energy rule from January 21, 2025 that set new energy standards for commercial refrigerators, freezers, and refrigerator-freezers
The rule would have made commercial refrigeration equipment use less electricity while still working properly
Manufacturers would not have to meet the new energy requirements for commercial refrigeration equipment

119-hjres-87.one_liner: Cancel EPA permission for California to set stricter truck emission standards
provisions: Cancels EPA permission from April 2023 that lets California set stricter emission rules for heavy-duty trucks
Removes California's ability to require longer warranties on truck pollution control equipment than federal law requires
Blocks California rules requiring some new trucks to produce zero emissions
Stops California from requiring airport shuttles to be zero-emission vehicles

119-hjres-88.one_liner: Cancel an EPA decision that lets California set its own car emission standards
provisions: Cancels the EPA's January 6, 2025 decision that let California create its own car emission rules
Stops California from implementing its Advanced Clean Cars II regulations
Requires California to follow federal emission standards for cars instead of setting its own

119-hjres-89.one_liner: Block California from requiring stricter pollution controls on heavy trucks than federal rules
provisions: Cancels the EPA's approval that lets California set stricter pollution limits on heavy trucks and buses than federal rules require
Stops new rules that would cut nitrogen oxide pollution from trucks by requiring cleaner engines
Prevents California from enforcing tougher emission standards that truck companies would have to meet when selling vehicles in California
Returns California to following only federal pollution limits for heavy-duty vehicles instead of stricter state standards

119-hr-1.one_liner: Cut taxes for families and businesses while raising the debt limit by $4 trillion
provisions: Makes permanent the individual tax rates of 10%, 12%, 22%, 24%, 32%, 35%, and 37% that were set to expire
Ends government loans that pay student interest and unlimited borrowing loans for graduate students and parents starting July 2026
Requires able-bodied adults ages 18-64 without young children to work 80 hours per month to get SNAP food benefits starting 2029
Requires states to pay 5% to 25% of SNAP food benefit costs based on their error rates starting 2028
Cuts funding for electric vehicle tax credits and many climate programs while adding $100 billion for military spending
Requires adults in Medicaid expansion states to work 80 hours per month starting 2029 to keep their health coverage
Raises the government borrowing limit by $4 trillion to avoid default on federal debt

119-hr-1968.one_liner: Fund the federal government through September 2025
provisions: Funds government programs at 2024 spending levels through September 2025
Prevents a government shutdown when current funding ends March 14, 2025
Extends the National Flood Insurance Program that covers flood damage
Extends Medicare and Medicaid health programs
Extends Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF) program that gives cash to families
Extends immigration enforcement programs
Keeps fentanyl-related drugs classified as illegal controlled substances

119-hr-23.one_liner: Punish anyone helping International Criminal Court prosecute Americans and allies
provisions: Punishes foreign persons who help the International Criminal Court prosecute Americans or people from US ally countries
Blocks visas and freezes property of people who assist with court investigations or arrests
Cuts all US funding to the International Criminal Court and stops future payments
Also blocks visas for immediate family members of people who help the court

119-hr-3944.one_liner: Fund military construction and veterans programs for the year starting October 2025
provisions: Funds military construction projects for the Army, Navy, Marine Corps, Air Force, and reserves
Funds Department of Veterans Affairs programs including health care, benefits, and cemeteries
Funds military family housing construction and maintenance
Funds NATO Security Investment Program
Funds American Battle Monuments Commission and Armed Forces Retirement Home

119-hr-4.one_liner: Cancel $9.4 billion in unspent funding for foreign aid and public broadcasting
provisions: Takes back $9.4 billion that was approved for government agencies but not yet spent
Cancels money for health programs, refugee assistance, and peacekeeping in other countries
Removes funding for programs that support democracy and provide disaster aid worldwide
Cuts money for the Corporation for Public Broadcasting
Stops funding for clean technology programs and development assistance

119-s-1582.one_liner: Create federal rules for digital stablecoins and require companies to back them with real money
provisions: Creates rules for digital coins called stablecoins that companies promise to exchange for a fixed dollar amount
Allows only banks or companies with federal or state permission to issue these coins to Americans
Requires companies to keep real money or similar assets to back each digital coin on a one-to-one basis
Requires companies to publish monthly reports showing what assets back their coins
Lets companies choose federal or state rules, but limits state rules to companies issuing $10 billion or less
Allows foreign companies to sell these coins in America if Treasury approves their home country's rules
Makes companies follow anti-money laundering rules but exempts the coins from securities laws

119-s-2296.one_liner: Set military spending limits and defense policies for 2026
provisions: Sets spending limits for the military for the year starting October 2025 but does not actually give the money
Lets the military buy planes, ships, and missiles
Sets how many active duty and reserve troops the military can have
Approves military construction projects
Requires the Defense Department to create a plan about biotech security threats
Continues a program to strengthen military readiness in the Pacific region through 2026
Removes diversity and inclusion programs from the military including the Chief Diversity Officer job

119-s-331.one_liner: Make all fentanyl-type drugs permanently illegal with set penalties
provisions: Makes all fentanyl-type drugs permanently illegal as the most controlled type of drug
Sets penalties of 10 years minimum in prison for crimes involving 100 grams or more of fentanyl-type drugs
Creates a new way for researchers to get permission to study these banned drugs
Lets researchers use one permit for multiple research locations instead of separate permits
Allows researchers to skip new safety inspections in some cases
Lets approved researchers make small amounts of banned drugs for studies without extra permits

119-s-5.one_liner: Require detention of non-citizens charged with stealing and let states sue over immigration decisions
provisions: Requires the government to detain non-citizens who are charged with, arrested for, or convicted of break-ins, stealing, or shoplifting
Applies to people in the country without permission or who did not have proper documents when entering
Lets states sue the federal government when immigration decisions harm the state by more than $100
States can sue when the government releases someone from detention
States can sue when the government fails to properly check people entering the country or seeking asylum
States can sue when the government fails to detain someone who has been ordered to leave the country

119-s-6.one_liner: Require medical care for children born alive during abortions
provisions: Requires doctors to give the same medical care to babies born alive during abortions as other newborn babies
Requires doctors to send these babies to hospitals right away
Makes doctors report other doctors who don't follow these rules to police
Creates criminal penalties of fines and up to 5 years in prison for doctors who don't comply
Makes intentionally killing a baby born alive during an abortion count as murder
Protects mothers from being charged with crimes under this law
Lets mothers sue doctors who break these rules

119-s-9.one_liner: Ban people born male from women's sports at federally funded schools
provisions: Bans people born male from playing in women's and girls' sports at schools that get federal money
Makes this against Title IX rules that schools must follow
Defines sex based on biology and genetics at birth
Applies to public elementary schools, high schools, and colleges

119-sjres-10.one_liner: Cancel the national emergency about energy declared on January 20, 2025
provisions: Ends the national emergency about energy that started on January 20, 2025
Removes emergency powers that let agencies approve energy projects faster
Stops agencies from using emergency rules to skip normal environmental permits
Ends the Defense Department's emergency authority to build energy infrastructure
Removes the requirement for the Defense Department to assess energy vulnerabilities

119-sjres-11.one_liner: Cancel a rule requiring archaeological surveys for offshore oil and gas drilling
provisions: Cancels a rule requiring oil and gas companies to survey the sea floor for historical items before offshore drilling
The rule made companies look for old objects and human remains that are at least 50 years old
Before this rule, companies only had to do surveys when the government thought historical items might be present
The cancelled rule required these surveys for all offshore drilling projects

119-sjres-12.one_liner: Cancel an EPA rule charging oil and gas companies fees for methane emissions
provisions: Cancels an EPA rule published in November 2024
Stops the government from charging oil and gas companies annual fees for methane emissions
Companies would no longer pay fees when they emit methane above set limits

119-sjres-13.one_liner: Cancel a Treasury rule that ended automatic approvals for some bank mergers
provisions: Cancels a Treasury Department rule published in September 2024 about bank mergers
Restores automatic approval for some bank merger requests under fast review
Brings back shorter application forms for banks wanting to merge
Returns bank merger review process to how it worked before September 2024

119-sjres-18.one_liner: Cancel a rule that would cap overdraft fees at $5 at large banks
provisions: Cancels a Consumer Financial Protection Bureau rule about overdraft fees at large banks
Blocks a rule that would cap overdraft charges at $5 per overdraft
Stops a rule that would let banks charge more than $5 only with written justification
Prevents a rule that would make banks treat overdrafts like credit cards with disclosure requirements

119-sjres-26.one_liner: Block a proposed military sale to Israel
provisions: Stops a proposed military sale to Israel from going through
Blocks the sale of defense articles and services to Israel
Uses Congress's power to disapprove foreign military sales before they happen

119-sjres-28.one_liner: Cancel a rule defining which payment apps get federal oversight
provisions: Cancels a rule that said payment apps with 50 million or more transactions per year must follow federal oversight
Removes the rule that defined which digital payment companies fall under government supervision
Affects payment apps like Venmo, PayPal, and Cash App that handle large numbers of transactions

119-sjres-3.one_liner: Cancel an IRS rule requiring crypto brokers to report digital asset sales
provisions: Cancels an IRS rule issued on December 30, 2024 about reporting crypto transactions
Stops crypto brokers that handle decentralized finance transactions from having to report sales to the IRS
Removes the requirement for these platforms to send information about digital asset sales to tax authorities

119-sjres-31.one_liner: Cancel an EPA air pollution rule from September 2024
provisions: Cancels an EPA rule published September 10, 2024
The rule affects facilities that release toxic air pollutants that stay in the environment and build up in animals and people
These facilities must keep following strict pollution limits even when they reduce emissions enough to qualify for looser limits
Canceling the rule lets facilities switch to looser pollution limits when they cut emissions

119-sjres-33.one_liner: Block a proposed weapons sale to Israel
provisions: Stops a planned sale of weapons and military equipment to Israel
Uses a congressional vote to block the sale from happening
Applies to defense articles and services that were proposed for sale

119-sjres-34.one_liner: Block a proposed military weapons sale to Israel
provisions: Stops the US government from completing one specific planned sale of military equipment to Israel
Prevents weapons and defense services from being transferred to Israel under this particular deal
Blocks only this one sale, not all future military sales to Israel

119-sjres-37.one_liner: End the national emergency that put tariffs on Canadian imports
provisions: Ends the national emergency declared on February 1, 2025
Removes the 25% tariff on most imports from Canada
Removes the 10% tariff on Canadian energy like oil and gas

119-sjres-41.one_liner: Block the sale of certain military weapons to Israel
provisions: Blocks the U.S. from selling certain military weapons to Israel
Stops a weapons sale that the government had planned

119-sjres-49.one_liner: End the national emergency that imposed 10% tariffs on imports
provisions: Ends the national emergency declared by President Trump on April 2, 2025
Stops the 10% tariff on most imports coming into the United States
Removes additional tariffs on imports from certain countries

119-sjres-53.one_liner: Block the sale of weapons and military equipment to Qatar
provisions: Blocks a proposed sale of weapons and military equipment to Qatar
Stops military services from being sold to Qatar
Uses Congress's power to reject weapons sales to other countries

119-sjres-54.one_liner: Block a military weapons sale to the United Arab Emirates
provisions: Stops a planned sale of military weapons and equipment to the United Arab Emirates
Uses Congress's power to disapprove foreign arms sales approved by the executive branch
Prevents the UAE government from receiving the defense items in this proposed deal

119-sjres-55.one_liner: Cancel a federal rule setting safety standards for hydrogen-powered cars
provisions: Cancels a rule from the federal car safety agency about hydrogen-powered vehicles
Removes safety standards for fuel systems in cars that run on hydrogen instead of gasoline
Stops rules about how hydrogen storage containers must be built in these cars

119-sjres-59.one_liner: Stop military action against Iran without congressional approval
provisions: Tells the President to end any current U.S. military action against Iran
Requires Congress to approve any future military action against Iran
Says Congress must either declare war on Iran or pass a specific law allowing military force

119-sjres-7.one_liner: Cancel an FCC rule that allowed schools to buy discounted Wi-Fi hotspots for students
provisions: Cancels an FCC rule from August 2024 about the E-Rate program for schools and libraries
The cancelled rule allowed schools and libraries to buy discounted Wi-Fi hotspots through E-Rate
Students, staff, and library users could take these hotspots home for homework and educational use
After this bill passes, schools and libraries cannot buy these discounted hotspots anymore

119-hconres-14.one_liner: Set federal budget targets for 2025-2034 and order committees to write deficit-changing laws
provisions: Sets budget targets for fiscal years 2025-2034 (October 2024 through September 2034)
Orders 11 House committees to write laws that change the deficit by March 27, 2025
Lets these laws pass the Senate without filibuster using special reconciliation rules
Allows the debt limit to increase by up to $4.5 trillion
Requires committees to find at least $2 trillion in deficit cuts or the debt limit increase gets reduced
Sets spending targets for different government functions like defense and healthcare

119-hconres-30.one_liner: Express support for local law enforcement officers
provisions: States Congress recognizes local law enforcement officers for their work protecting communities
States Congress thanks local law enforcement officers and their families for their service
States Congress honors local law enforcement officers for their sacrifices

119-hjres-104.one_liner: Cancel a federal rule that blocks coal leasing on 1.7 million acres in Montana
provisions: Cancels a Bureau of Land Management rule issued on November 20, 2024
The rule blocked coal leasing on 1.7 million acres of federal land in Montana
Makes those acres available for coal companies to consider for leasing again
Applies to land managed by the Miles City Field Office in Montana

119-hjres-105.one_liner: Cancel a Bureau of Land Management rule limiting oil and gas development in North Dakota
provisions: Cancels a Bureau of Land Management rule issued on January 8, 2025
The rule limited oil and gas drilling in North Dakota areas unlikely to have much oil or gas
The rule limited new coal mining to areas within four miles of existing mines
The rule updated a 1988 plan that guides how the government manages federal land in North Dakota

119-hjres-106.one_liner: Cancel a federal rule on 3.6 million acres in Alaska
provisions: Cancels a Bureau of Land Management rule for Alaska's Central Yukon area
The rule designated 21 environmental areas covering 3.6 million acres
The rule was issued on November 12, 2024

119-hjres-78.one_liner: Cancel a rule that protects longfin smelt fish as endangered
provisions: Cancels a Fish and Wildlife Service rule from July 30, 2024
That rule listed longfin smelt fish in San Francisco Bay as endangered
Removes endangered species protections for this fish
Uses congressional review to overturn the agency rule

119-hr-1039.one_liner: Require VA to review letters sent to veterans and extend loan fees through 2034
provisions: Requires the VA to hire a research center to review letters sent to veterans applying for benefits
VA must report what the review finds and make changes based on the recommendations
Extends current loan fee rates for VA home loans through June 23, 2034

119-hr-1048.one_liner: Require colleges to report smaller foreign gifts and restrict contracts with China and Russia
provisions: Lowers the amount colleges must report for foreign gifts from $250,000 to $50,000
Makes colleges report any gift from China, Russia, or other countries of concern, no matter the amount
Stops colleges from making contracts with China, Russia, or other countries of concern without getting a government waiver first
Makes colleges report if foreign sources control them
Makes college researchers report their foreign gifts and contracts
Makes private colleges with large assets file yearly investment reports
Sets penalties including loss of federal student aid for colleges that break these rules

119-hr-1156.one_liner: Extend time limits for prosecuting fraud in COVID unemployment programs from 5 to 10 years
provisions: Extends time limits from 5 to 10 years for prosecuting people who committed fraud in special unemployment programs created during COVID
Applies to programs that gave unemployment benefits to gig workers, self-employed people, and others who normally cannot get unemployment
Covers both criminal charges and fraud lawsuits against people who filed fake claims
Takes back unused anti-fraud money that was given to the Labor Department in 2021
Does not apply if the 5-year time limit already expired before this bill becomes law

119-hr-1223.one_liner: Require the National Science Foundation to plan cybersecurity upgrades for research ships
provisions: Requires the National Science Foundation to write a plan for upgrading internet and cybersecurity on research ships
Research ships are floating labs where scientists and students study the ocean
The plan must say how much upgrades will cost and how long they will take
The plan must be sent to Congress within 18 months
The National Science Foundation must later report to Congress on how the upgrades are going

119-hr-1228.one_liner: Move the Office of Survivors Assistance to report directly to the VA Secretary
provisions: Moves the Office of Survivors Assistance from the Veterans Benefits Administration to the Office of the Secretary
The office currently operates under the Pension and Fiduciary Service within the Veterans Benefits Administration
After this change, the office will report directly to the VA Secretary instead of through multiple layers
The Office of Survivors Assistance helps families of veterans who have died
This changes how the office is managed within the Department of Veterans Affairs but does not change what services it provides

119-hr-1263.one_liner: Create a working group with Japan, Australia and India for security and economic cooperation
provisions: Tells the State Department to set up a working group with Japan, Australia, and India
Requires the State Department to write a strategy report to Congress about cooperation with these three countries
The report must list current and past joint projects on economic growth, technology, energy, and supply chains
The report must recommend how Congress can help remove barriers to cooperation
The report must recommend what new abilities the U.S. needs to lead joint projects
The report must recommend additional money and resources needed to expand successful projects

119-hr-1286.one_liner: Make the VA get help fixing their veteran claim forms
provisions: The VA must hire a research center to review the forms they send to veterans filing claims
The research center will recommend ways to make the forms clearer and better organized
The VA must make some of these recommended changes within 2 years

119-hr-1326.one_liner: Require the Department of Energy and Department of Agriculture to work together on research projects
provisions: Requires the Department of Energy and Department of Agriculture to work together on research through a written agreement
Creates a contest where federal agencies, national labs, universities, and nonprofits can apply for research funding
Focuses research on machine learning, biofuels, power grid updates, rural technology, and wildfire prevention
Lets the departments share research costs with each other
Supports building research facilities and training workers
Studies ways to make farming work better and reduce greenhouse gas pollution

119-hr-1402.one_liner: Require ticket sellers to show total prices upfront and ban selling tickets they don't have
provisions: Makes ticket sellers show the full ticket price when tickets are first displayed to buyers
Forces sellers to list the base ticket price and each fee separately before completing a purchase
Makes total prices appear in all ads and marketing for tickets
Bans selling tickets that sellers don't actually own or control
Lets secondary sellers offer services to find tickets if they clearly say it's a service, not a ticket
Makes sellers give full refunds when events are canceled or postponed
Gives the Federal Trade Commission power to enforce these rules

119-hr-144.one_liner: Require the Tennessee Valley Authority to report finances and high salaries to Congress
provisions: Brings back a requirement for the Tennessee Valley Authority to send annual financial reports to Congress each March
Changes which employee salaries must be reported from those earning more than $1,500 per year to those earning more than about $140,000 per year
Requires reporting the names, salaries, and job duties of high-earning employees

119-hr-1442.one_liner: Ban consumer products containing 10% or more sodium nitrite
provisions: Makes consumer products with 10% or more sodium nitrite banned hazardous products
Does not apply to drugs and medical devices
Does not apply to cosmetics and food products

119-hr-1491.one_liner: Make disaster deadline delays count as tax extensions
provisions: Makes disaster deadline delays count the same as regular deadline extensions for tax refunds
Changes how the IRS calculates tax refund amounts when disasters postpone filing deadlines
Includes disaster delays when the IRS figures out how much to refund taxpayers
Changes when the IRS can send tax payment notices during disasters

119-hr-1503.one_liner: Block property and visas of people who harvest organs by force
provisions: Requires the President to make a list of people and groups who take organs by force or help traffic people for organ harvesting
Blocks those people from getting US visas and freezes any property they own in America
Lets the State Department deny or take away passports from Americans convicted of selling human organs for money
Only applies to passport denial if the person is in prison or on probation and used a passport or crossed a border during the crime

119-hr-152.one_liner: Require FEMA to make disaster aid applications easier and study damage assessments
provisions: Requires FEMA to study how to make disaster aid applications less time-consuming and less repetitive for people applying for help
Requires FEMA to create a plan to regularly track and report how much federal disaster aid money gets awarded
Requires FEMA to form a working group that meets regularly to find duplicate work in damage assessments after disasters
Requires the working group to study whether one federal agency should do all damage assessments instead of multiple agencies
Requires the working group to study using drones and other new technology to speed up damage assessments
Requires FEMA to report its findings to Congress within two years and post the report on FEMA's website for the public to read

119-hr-1526.one_liner: Limit federal court orders that block government rules nationwide
provisions: Federal district courts can currently issue orders blocking government rules nationwide
This bill would require court orders to only affect the specific people in the lawsuit
Courts could no longer stop government policies nationwide from a single case

119-hr-153.one_liner: Require federal agencies to publish quarterly reports on disaster assistance spending
provisions: Requires federal agencies that give disaster assistance to publish reports every three months
Reports must show the total amount of money the agency gave out for disasters
Reports must show how much of that money was actually spent
Reports must list all projects and activities that used the disaster money
Makes this information available to the public online
Applies to the Small Business Administration, Housing and Urban Development, and other agencies that provide disaster help

119-hr-1534.one_liner: Fund research into cement, concrete, and asphalt that produce less pollution
provisions: Creates a 7-year research program at the Department of Energy
Program funds projects to make cement, concrete, and asphalt that release less pollution
Department of Energy picks government agencies, nonprofits, schools, and companies for test projects
Projects focus on capturing pollution and using less energy when making these materials
Department can stop funding projects early if materials that create less pollution become available at normal prices
Program ends 7 years after the bill becomes law

119-hr-164.one_liner: Let electric companies do disaster prevention work when getting federal emergency help to restore power
provisions: Lets electric companies use federal emergency money to both fix power outages and strengthen power lines against future storms
Allows electric companies to get FEMA money to protect the same power facilities that are getting emergency repair help

119-hr-1642.one_liner: Require business centers to tell small businesses about hiring career school graduates
provisions: Requires Small Business Development Centers and Women's Business Centers to tell small businesses about hiring career school graduates
Lets these centers connect small businesses with career schools to help students and graduates find jobs

119-hr-165.one_liner: Place 40 acres in South Dakota into special tribal ownership for two Sioux tribes
provisions: Places 40 acres in Oglala Lakota County, South Dakota into special tribal ownership for the Oglala Sioux Tribe and Cheyenne River Sioux Tribe
Under this ownership, the tribes own the land but cannot sell it or borrow money against it without federal approval
Requires the land to be maintained as a memorial and sacred site
Blocks any commercial development or casinos on the land
This bill requires a study of cybersecurity risks in mobile phone networks.
The study must examine threats to the networks that carry cell phone calls, texts, and data.
Recommendations must be provided to Congress on how to improve mobile network security.
119-hr-1709.one_liner: This bill requires a study of cybersecurity risks in mobile phone networks.

119-hr-1717.one_liner: Give legal power to a council advising FCC on network safety
provisions: Creates legal authority for a council that advises the FCC on communications network security and reliability
Requires council members from communications companies, public interest groups, and federal, state, tribal, and local governments
Bars council membership for representatives of companies controlled by foreign adversaries or that pose national security threats
Removes the normal 2-year limit that ends government advisory groups, letting this council continue indefinitely

119-hr-1770.one_liner: Create a pilot program using artificial intelligence to help track unsafe consumer products
provisions: Creates a pilot program for the Consumer Product Safety Commission to use artificial intelligence
The commission can use AI to track injury trends from consumer products
The commission can use AI to find dangerous products
The commission can use AI to watch for recalled products still being sold
The commission can use AI to find imported products that don't meet safety rules
Makes the Commerce Department write a report about using blockchain technology for consumer protection
Makes the Federal Trade Commission write a report about stopping unfair practices with digital tokens

119-hr-1804.one_liner: Require yearly reports on agents who help with small business loans
provisions: Requires the Small Business Administration to publish a yearly report about agents who help people apply for government-backed small business loans
The report must count how many agents work with loan applicants and what types of services they provide
The report must count fraudulent loans that involved these agents
The report must track loans where agents helped with applications
The report must show the total dollar amount paid to agents in referral fees

119-hr-186.one_liner: Require the Medal of Honor monument to be built in the main National Mall area
provisions: Requires an approved monument honoring Medal of Honor recipients to be built in the main National Mall area
The main National Mall area runs from the Capitol to the Lincoln Memorial and from the White House to the Jefferson Memorial

119-hr-187.one_liner: Create online maps showing where people can fish and boat on federal waters
provisions: Requires the Forest Service and Interior Department to create shared rules for collecting information about rivers and lakes on federal land
Agencies must put maps online showing where people can fish, boat, and access waterways within 5 years
Agencies must update waterway access information at least twice each year
Agencies must update fishing rules online right away when changes happen
Creates a way for people to ask questions or give feedback about the waterway information

119-hr-1917.one_liner: Make official a federal program that marks hatchery fish in the Great Lakes
provisions: Makes official a federal program that marks hatchery fish in the Great Lakes
The program has been running since 2010 but did not have legal authority
Marks help show which fish came from hatcheries and which are wild
Lets the Fish and Wildlife Service buy equipment for marking more fish
Lets the agency hire more workers for the program
Requires sharing fish data with state and tribal fish managers

119-hr-1919.one_liner: Stop the Federal Reserve from creating a digital dollar
provisions: Stops Federal Reserve banks from offering banking services directly to people
Stops the Federal Reserve from holding bank accounts for individuals
Stops the Federal Reserve from creating a digital dollar
Stops the Federal Reserve from using digital currency to set interest rates
Stops the Federal Reserve from testing or studying digital currency, with some exceptions allowed

119-hr-192.one_liner: Require Amtrak to publicly report executive pay and bonus amounts
provisions: Amtrak must report the base pay of each executive in its yearly report to Congress
Amtrak must report all bonus money paid to executives
The report must explain how bonuses are decided and what goals executives must meet
Amtrak must post this report on its public website

119-hr-1998.one_liner: Block visas and freeze property of people who commit piracy
provisions: The President must stop pirates from getting visas to enter the US
The President must freeze the money and property that pirates own in the US
People who break these new rules can be fined or sent to jail
These rules apply to foreign individuals and groups that commit piracy

119-hr-2035.one_liner: Require all Department of Transportation cargo to be shipped on U.S.-flagged vessels
provisions: Changes the rule from 50% to 100% of federal cargo that must be shipped on U.S.-flagged vessels
Applies to all cargo the Department of Transportation buys or finances
Covers cargo DOT contracts for itself and cargo it pays others to ship
Ships must be available at fair and reasonable prices
Must allow fair participation of U.S.-flagged ships across different regions

119-hr-2056.one_liner: Require Washington D.C. to cooperate with federal immigration enforcement
provisions: Stops Washington D.C. from limiting cooperation with federal immigration enforcement
Requires D.C. to share information about people's citizenship and immigration status with federal agencies
Requires D.C. to hold non-citizens in custody for up to 48 hours when federal immigration agents request it
Allows D.C. to refuse these requirements for crime victims and witnesses

119-hr-2096.one_liner: Change rules for disciplining DC police officers
provisions: Brings back a 90-day time limit for starting discipline cases against DC police officers
Lets police unions negotiate discipline rules in their work contracts
Stops the DC police chief from making penalties harsher than what police review boards recommend
Ends the requirement to post online schedules of discipline hearings for officers being fired

119-hr-21.one_liner: Require medical care for babies born alive after abortions
provisions: Requires doctors to give babies born alive after abortions the same medical care as any other baby their age
Requires doctors to send babies born alive after abortions to a hospital right away
Requires doctors and hospital staff to report to police if someone does not follow these rules
Sets fines and up to 5 years in prison for doctors who do not follow these rules
Makes killing a baby born alive after an abortion count as murder
Does not let mothers be charged with crimes under this law
Lets mothers sue doctors who break these rules

119-hr-22.one_liner: Require documentary proof of U.S. citizenship to register to vote in federal elections
provisions: Stops states from accepting voter registration forms unless the person shows documents proving U.S. citizenship
Accepts REAL ID driver's licenses and other government documents that show citizenship
Requires states to create backup processes for people who cannot provide these documents
Requires states to find and remove non-citizens from voter registration lists
Makes it a crime to register someone to vote without checking their citizenship documents
Lets people file lawsuits against election officials who break these rules

119-hr-2215.one_liner: Rename Salem Maritime National Historic Site to Salem Maritime National Historical Park
provisions: Changes the name of Salem Maritime National Historic Site in Massachusetts to Salem Maritime National Historical Park
Orders the Department of the Interior to study the park's boundaries
The study will look at whether to add sites with maritime history, coastal defenses, and military history to the park
Requires the Interior Department to write a report on the boundary study

119-hr-2240.one_liner: Require the Justice Department to report on attacks against police officers and mental health resources
provisions: Requires the Justice Department to write a report on attacks against law enforcement officers
Requires the Justice Department to study whether these attacks can be tracked in existing crime reporting systems
Requires the Justice Department to report on mental health resources available to law enforcement officers

119-hr-2243.one_liner: Expand where law enforcement officers can carry concealed guns across state lines
provisions: Lets active and retired law enforcement officers carry concealed guns in school zones
Lets these officers carry concealed guns in national parks
Lets these officers carry concealed guns on state, local, or private property open to the public
Lets these officers carry concealed guns in federal buildings open to the public
Lets states reduce how often retired officers must meet shooting and training requirements
This bill allows federal law enforcement officers to buy their service weapons when they retire.
Officers must pay fair market value for the firearm.
The agency must approve the sale and the officer must pass a background check.
119-hr-2255.one_liner: This bill allows federal law enforcement officers to buy their service weapons when they retire.

119-hr-2483.one_liner: Continue funding programs that treat drug addiction and mental health through 2030
provisions: Continues funding for programs that help pregnant women and new mothers with drug problems from October 2025 through September 2030
Continues funding for programs that help young people avoid and recover from drug problems from October 2025 through September 2030
Continues funding for housing for people recovering from drug problems from October 2025 through September 2030
Continues funding to pay back student loans for doctors and nurses who treat drug problems from October 2025 through September 2030
Lets first responders buy medicines to treat overdoses from drugs other than opioids
Lets job programs for people in recovery also pay for transportation to work
Requires the government to protect the suicide prevention hotline from cyber attacks

119-hr-249.one_liner: Rename trail bridge and park at Paterson Great Falls after Bill Pascrell Jr
provisions: Renames the Great Falls Scenic Overlook Trail Bridge to the Bill Pascrell Jr. Scenic Overlook Trail Bridge
Renames Overlook Park to Bill Pascrell Jr. Overlook Park
Changes affect facilities at Paterson Great Falls National Historical Park in New Jersey

119-hr-26.one_liner: Block the President from banning fracking without Congress approval
provisions: Blocks the President from temporarily stopping fracking unless Congress votes to allow it
Says states should control fracking rules on state and private land

119-hr-27.one_liner: Make fentanyl-related substances permanently illegal under federal drug laws
provisions: Makes all fentanyl-related substances permanently illegal as Schedule I drugs, the most restricted category
Sets prison sentences of at least 10 years for people caught with 100 grams or more of these substances
Creates a new process for researchers to legally study these substances
Lets researchers use one permit for multiple research locations under certain conditions
Skips inspection requirements in some cases for research projects
Lets researchers make small amounts of substances for research without getting a separate manufacturing permit

119-hr-275.one_liner: Require monthly reports on non-citizens trying to enter illegally who pose security risks
provisions: Makes the Department of Homeland Security write a report every month
The report covers non-citizens who try to enter the US illegally and might pose security risks
The report must include how many people were caught
The report must include what countries they came from or lived in last
The report must include where border agents found them

119-hr-276.one_liner: Rename the Gulf of Mexico to the Gulf of America
provisions: Renames the Gulf of Mexico to the Gulf of America
Requires federal agencies to update their documents to use the name Gulf of America
Requires federal agencies to update their maps to use the name Gulf of America

119-hr-28.one_liner: Ban students born male from competing in women's and girls' sports at federally funded schools
provisions: Makes it illegal under federal education law for federally funded schools to let students born male compete in sports programs for women or girls
Defines sex based on reproductive biology and genetics at birth
Lets male students train and practice with women's and girls' teams as long as no female student loses opportunities
Orders the Government Accountability Office to write a report on effects of male participation in women's sports

119-hr-29.one_liner: Require detention of non-citizens charged or convicted of theft and break-ins
provisions: Requires the government to detain non-citizens who are charged with, arrested for, convicted of, or admit to burglary, theft, larceny, or shoplifting
Applies to people who are in the country illegally or did not have proper documents when entering
Lets states sue the federal government over immigration decisions that harm the state or its residents
States can sue if the financial harm is more than $100
States can sue over releasing non-citizens from custody, failing to do required asylum interviews, or not following immigration rules

119-hr-2931.one_liner: Move Small Business Administration offices out of cities that limit cooperation with immigration enforcement
provisions: Requires the Small Business Administration to move its offices out of states and cities that limit cooperation with immigration enforcement
Gives the agency 60 days to move an office after deciding it's in one of these places
Stops the Small Business Administration from opening new offices in these states and cities
Applies to places that stop their workers from sharing citizenship information with federal agencies or following Department of Homeland Security requests

119-hr-2966.one_liner: Restrict Small Business Administration loans to US citizens and permanent residents only
provisions: Requires Small Business Administration loan applications to include birth dates
Requires applicants to certify they are US citizens or permanent residents
Stops asylum seekers and refugees from getting SBA business loans
Stops people on temporary visas from getting SBA business loans
Stops DACA recipients from getting SBA business loans
Stops people without legal immigration status from getting SBA business loans

119-hr-2987.one_liner: Cap for-profit companies that can make government-backed small business loans at 16
provisions: Limits the number of for-profit small business lending companies to 16 that can make government-backed loans at any time
Applies to SBA 7(a) loans, which help small businesses get funding with government backing
Prevents more than 16 for-profit lending companies from participating in this loan program at one time

119-hr-30.one_liner: Expand the crimes that can block non-citizens from entering or get them deported
provisions: Blocks non-citizens from entering the US if they committed stalking, child abuse, sex crimes, or domestic violence
Adds sex crimes as grounds for deporting non-citizens
Expands domestic violence crimes that can lead to deportation to include controlling behavior in close relationships

119-hr-3095.one_liner: Require USPS to give 74 communities their own ZIP codes
provisions: Requires the U.S. Postal Service to create a single ZIP code for each of 74 specific communities
These communities currently share ZIP codes with other areas or do not have their own ZIP codes
The bill names all 74 communities, which are located in 22 states

119-hr-33.one_liner: Change tax rules for Taiwan residents who earn money from US sources
provisions: Creates special tax rules for Taiwan residents who earn income in the United States
Changes the tax rate on interest, dividends, and royalties from 30 percent to a lower rate for Taiwan residents
Changes rules about what counts as having a US business for Taiwan residents
Applies to income like bank interest, stock dividends, and payments for using ideas or inventions

119-hr-3351.one_liner: Remove paperwork rules for an SEC office that helps small businesses get funding
provisions: Removes federal paperwork requirements for the Office of the Advocate for Small Business Capital Formation
This SEC office helps small businesses raise money through the stock market
The office can now collect information from people without following certain government procedures
The office must still tell people why it is collecting their information

119-hr-3357.one_liner: Require companies with unequal voting shares to disclose ownership details
provisions: Requires companies that give some shareholders more voting power than others to report ownership details
Companies must tell shareholders how many shares each director and executive owns
Companies must tell shareholders how much voting power each director and executive has
Companies must report the same information for any person who owns 5% or more of the voting shares
This information must be included when companies ask shareholders to vote on company matters

119-hr-3394.one_liner: Let more people invest in private company deals based on professional knowledge
provisions: Changes rules so people with professional investment knowledge can invest in private company deals that are currently limited to wealthy people
Makes it official law that licensed brokers and investment advisers can invest in these deals
Makes it official law that people with high salaries or high net worth can invest in these deals
Orders the SEC to update investment rules to match these changes

119-hr-3422.one_liner: Expand an SEC office to help small businesses raise money
provisions: Expands what an SEC office can do to help small businesses raise money
Requires the office to provide learning materials for small businesses trying to get funding
Requires the office to host events for underrepresented small businesses, rural businesses, and businesses hit by disasters
Requires the office to meet once a year with state officials to coordinate

119-hr-35.one_liner: Make fleeing from Border Patrol agents near the border a federal crime
provisions: Makes it a federal crime to drive away from Border Patrol agents within 100 miles of the U.S. border
Sets mandatory minimum prison sentences if someone dies or gets hurt during the chase
Makes non-citizens convicted of this crime deportable and bars them from getting asylum
Applies to fleeing from any federal, state, or local police officer helping Border Patrol

119-hr-359.one_liner: Require the Department of Energy to report on when it reduces cost requirements for energy projects
provisions: The Department of Energy must report when it reduces or eliminates requirements for companies and groups to contribute money to federally-funded energy projects
Reports must cover energy research, development, demonstration, and commercial projects
The first report is due within 120 days of the bill becoming law
New reports are due every three months after that

119-hr-36.one_liner: Impose sanctions on Georgian officials who block the country's NATO and EU membership
provisions: Blocks visas for Georgian officials who undermine their country's security or stability
Lets the President freeze assets of these officials
Targets Georgian parliament members serving since January 1, 2014
These officials must have used corruption or violence to block Georgia from joining NATO and the European Union
Applies sanctions to family members who benefited from the official's actions
Requires the President to identify corrupt Georgian officials for potential sanctions

119-hr-3633.one_liner: Create federal rules for companies that buy and sell digital assets
provisions: Makes the Commodity Futures Trading Commission regulate companies that trade digital assets
Sets rules for which digital assets can be sold on exchanges
Makes companies keep records and keep customer money separate from company money
Lets some digital assets skip Securities and Exchange Commission filing rules if sales stay under a set limit
Requires companies to follow money-tracking laws that banks must follow

119-hr-375.one_liner: Fund research and control efforts for a tree disease killing Hawaiian forests
provisions: Funds research on a disease that kills ohia trees, which are native to Hawaii
Requires federal agencies to work with Hawaii to stop the tree disease from spreading
Provides money to prevent the disease from reaching new forest areas
Funds efforts to replant Hawaiian forests damaged by the disease
Pays for scientists and research facilities to study how the disease spreads
Controls deer and other animals that might carry the disease on public and private land

119-hr-3937.one_liner: Sell 14 acres of federal forest land in Wisconsin to a concrete company
provisions: Requires the Forest Service to sell 14 acres of federal forest land in Wisconsin to Tony's Wabeno Redi-Mix company
The sale includes the rights to any minerals under the land
The sale price must equal the land's market value as set by a government appraisal
The company has 180 days after the appraisal is approved to make an offer to buy
The company must pay the market value plus all costs for the sale
Requires the Interior Department to review how the government approves stone, sand and gravel projects on federal land

119-hr-4016.one_liner: Fund the military for the year starting October 2025
provisions: Pays military troops their salaries and benefits
Covers daily costs to run military bases and maintain equipment
Buys new weapons and equipment and pays for military research
Funds healthcare for military members
Pays to destroy old chemical weapons and stop drug trafficking
Covers CIA worker retirement costs and intelligence management

119-hr-4216.one_liner: Review which military equipment can be sold directly by companies to foreign governments
provisions: Requires the State Department to review military equipment each year
Looks at equipment that can only be sold government-to-government
Checks if these items should also allow company-to-government sales
Compares how long each sales process takes
Studies the impact on State Department and Defense Department work
Reports results to Congress within 30 days of each review

119-hr-4275.one_liner: Authorize funding for the Coast Guard and create a Secretary of the Coast Guard position
provisions: Authorizes money for the Coast Guard to operate
Creates a new Secretary of the Coast Guard position to lead the service
Sets the number of Coast Guard members allowed and their training levels
Requires reports on Coast Guard ship and equipment purchases
Creates a Special Advisor position for working with tribal groups and Native Hawaiians
Brings back training for Coast Guard leaders on how Congress works
Sets rules for the National Coast Guard Museum's funding and operations

119-hr-43.one_liner: Let Alaska Native corporations keep land instead of giving it to Alaska
provisions: Removes the rule that Alaska Native village corporations must give certain lands to Alaska
Alaska currently holds this land in trust for future city governments that might be created
Lets Alaska Native corporations get back lands that Alaska currently holds for them
Corporations can get the land back by voting with village residents

119-hr-4553.one_liner: Fund energy and water agencies for the year starting October 2025
provisions: Funds the Army Corps of Engineers to build dams, manage rivers, and prevent floods
Funds Department of Energy programs to develop clean energy and improve the electrical grid
Funds nuclear weapons programs and nuclear power research
Funds water projects in western states through the Bureau of Reclamation
Funds agencies that watch over energy companies and nuclear power plants
Funds the government's emergency oil storage and energy security programs

119-hr-471.one_liner: Speed up forest management on federal land to prevent wildfires
provisions: Labels forest areas with wildfire risk as special management zones
Requires the Forest Service to create a center that tracks and predicts wildfires
Speeds up environmental reviews for forest management projects
Lets some forest work skip environmental reviews
Creates teams to speed up reviews under laws about endangered animals and historic sites
Makes it harder to sue over wildfire prevention projects
Funds forest restoration and wildfire prevention in communities

119-hr-495.one_liner: Require annual reports on border tunnel defense plans
provisions: Requires border patrol to report every year on tunnel defense plans
Changes current rule that requires only one report
Reports cover plans to stop illegal tunnels crossing the border

119-hr-517.one_liner: Let states request federal tax deadline extensions for disaster victims and double automatic extension time
provisions: Lets state governors ask the IRS to delay federal tax deadlines when the state declares a natural disaster
Increases automatic tax deadline extensions from 60 days to 120 days for people affected by federal disasters
Covers relief workers, people hurt or killed in disasters, and people whose homes or businesses are in disaster areas
Applies to natural disasters, fires, floods, and explosions that cause enough damage

119-hr-530.one_liner: Order a study of cancer rates among veterans who flew military planes
provisions: The VA must make an agreement with the National Academies of Sciences to do a study
The study looks at cancer rates among veterans who worked as crew members on military planes
It focuses on veterans who regularly flew in fixed-wing planes while serving in the military
The study examines how common cancers are in this group and how many die from cancer

119-hr-586.one_liner: Require the VA to study bile duct cancer rates in Vietnam veterans
provisions: Requires the VA to study bile duct cancer rates in veterans who served in Vietnam
Compares cancer rates between Vietnam veterans and regular Americans
Requires the VA to track bile duct cancer cases using their cancer registry
Requires the VA to send regular reports to Congress about the study results

119-hr-692.one_liner: Make U.S. officials push China to be more open about currency exchange rates
provisions: Tells U.S. officials at the International Monetary Fund to push China to be more open about how it sets its currency's value
Makes U.S. officials ask the IMF to watch China's currency policies more closely
Makes U.S. officials consider China's currency behavior when deciding China's voting power at the IMF
These rules stop working after 7 years and 30 days or if China becomes more open about its currency policies

119-hr-695.one_liner: Increase monthly payments to Medal of Honor recipients and their surviving spouses
provisions: Raises monthly payments to living Medal of Honor recipients from $1,407 to $8,333
Gives surviving spouses of Medal of Honor recipients $1,407 per month
Adjusts both payment amounts each year to keep up with inflation

119-hr-736.one_liner: Extend deadline for companies to file ownership information with government
provisions: Extends the deadline for companies to file ownership paperwork from January 1, 2025 to January 1, 2026
Applies to companies formed or registered before January 1, 2024
Companies must tell the government who actually owns and controls their business

119-hr-747.one_liner: Target Chinese companies and officials with sanctions for fentanyl trafficking
provisions: Adds Chinese companies that make fentanyl and similar drugs to the sanctions list if they don't stop drug trafficking
Adds senior Chinese government officials who help drug trafficking to the sanctions list
Requires the President to report to Congress each year through 2029 on who gets sanctioned for drug trafficking
Requires the President to study costs and benefits before making economic rules during drug trafficking emergencies

119-hr-758.one_liner: Require the Postal Service to track and report deaths from mail truck crashes
provisions: Requires the Postal Service to track deaths and injuries from crashes involving mail trucks
Mail workers and contractors must report crashes that hurt or kill people within 3 days
The Postal Service must keep a database of all crash injuries and deaths
The Postal Service must publish a yearly report about mail truck crashes

119-hr-77.one_liner: Let Congress reject multiple government rules at once from a President's final year
provisions: Currently Congress can only reject one government rule at a time with a single vote
Changes the law to let Congress reject multiple government rules with one vote
Only applies to rules made during a President's final year in office
Keeps the extra review time Congress already gets for rules made in the last 60 days

119-hr-776.one_liner: Extend federal funding for states to kill nutria and fix damaged marshlands through 2030
provisions: Extends a federal program that gives money to states to control nutria through the year ending September 2030
Nutria are large rodents from South America that damage wetlands and are not native to the U.S.
States can use federal money to kill or control these rodents
States can also use federal money to fix marshland that nutria have damaged

119-hr-788.one_liner: Require the Department of Energy and Small Business Administration to collaborate on research projects
provisions: Requires the Department of Energy and Small Business Administration to sign an agreement to work together on research projects
Requires these research projects to include small businesses when appropriate
Requires the agencies to write reports about their research results and ways to expand their work

119-hr-804.one_liner: Require the Small Business Administration to help rural disaster victims access federal aid
provisions: Requires the Small Business Administration to make sure rural disaster victims can access federal disaster aid
Tells the agency to do outreach and provide information materials to people in rural disaster areas

119-hr-818.one_liner: Require agencies to track first-time federal contracts with veteran and minority-owned small businesses
provisions: Requires federal agencies to count how many small businesses get their first federal contract each year
Tracks businesses owned by disabled veterans, minorities, and women
Adds this information to annual small business contracting scorecards agencies already file

119-hr-825.one_liner: Stop SBA assistance to people convicted of COVID loan fraud
provisions: Stops people convicted of fraud on COVID business loans from getting SBA money
Covers crimes like lying on Paycheck Protection Program loans and restaurant grants
Bans all SBA financial help except disaster loans
Also blocks help to businesses whose owners or key employees committed these crimes

119-hr-832.one_liner: Expand the Small Business Administration advocacy office to work with foreign governments
provisions: Expands the duties of the Small Business Administration advocacy office
Adds studying how small businesses work with other countries
Lets the office speak for small businesses to foreign governments about trade and regulation rules

119-hr-856.one_liner: Require a study of federal purchasing rules to find security risks
provisions: Tells the Office of Management and Budget to study how federal agencies buy things
The study looks at rules that make agencies pick the cheapest option that works
Checks if these buying rules create security risks for the country
The Office of Management and Budget must report what they find to Congress

119-hr-859.one_liner: Require companies to tell customers if smart devices have cameras or microphones
provisions: Companies must tell customers before they buy if a smart device has a camera or microphone
This applies to internet-connected devices like smart appliances
The rule does not apply to phones, laptops, or devices people expect to have cameras or microphones

119-hr-875.one_liner: Make drunk driving a reason to deny entry or remove non-citizens from the US
provisions: Makes drunk driving a reason to deny non-citizens entry into the United States
Allows removal of non-citizens convicted of drunk driving
Lets immigration officials deny entry to non-citizens who admit to drunk driving even without a conviction
Requires a conviction before someone can be removed from the US for drunk driving

119-hr-881.one_liner: Stop DHS from funding colleges that work with certain Chinese organizations
provisions: Stops the Department of Homeland Security from giving money to colleges that have contracts or agreements with Confucius Institutes
Stops DHS funding to colleges that work with China's Thousand Talents Program for technology and education
Stops DHS funding to colleges that received gifts or donations from Chinese universities involved in military, police, or intelligence work
Lets colleges get DHS funding back if they end these relationships with Chinese organizations
Defines Confucius Institutes as cultural programs funded by the Chinese government

119-hr-884.one_liner: Stop non-citizens from voting in Washington D.C. elections
provisions: Makes it illegal for non-citizens to vote in any Washington D.C. election
Cancels a 2022 D.C. law that let non-citizens vote in local elections
Removes voting rights for non-citizens that started on February 23, 2023

119-hr-901.one_liner: Require DHS to protect research from unauthorized access and audit disclosure compliance
provisions: Requires DHS to create a policy that protects research and development from being stolen or leaked
Orders the Government Accountability Office to audit whether DHS follows rules about disclosing conflicts of interest in government-funded research
The audit checks if DHS complies with 2021 rules requiring researchers to disclose ties to foreign governments

119-hr-973.one_liner: Create government safety rules for batteries in electric bikes and scooters
provisions: Requires the Consumer Product Safety Commission to write safety rules for batteries in electric bikes and scooters
Companies that make or sell these batteries must follow the new rules
Rules must use safety standards from three organizations

119-hr-993.one_liner: Require border agencies to write a plan for new technologies
provisions: Requires U.S. Customs and Border Protection to write a plan for using new technologies like AI and radar at the border
Lets the border agency create teams to find and test new commercial technologies
Requires the plan to include goals, timelines, and how to replace old technology programs
Requires the border agency to give the plan to Congress within 180 days
Requires the border agency to report to Congress every year about what the technology teams are doing

119-hr-997.one_liner: Let the National Taxpayer Advocate hire lawyers and manage all staff directly
provisions: Lets the National Taxpayer Advocate hire lawyers who report directly to them instead of to Treasury's legal department
Gives the National Taxpayer Advocate power to manage all employees in the Taxpayer Advocate Service
Changes the current system where the advocate could only manage local advocates in each state

119-hres-5.one_liner: Set operating rules for the House of Representatives for the 119th Congress
provisions: Requires 9 majority party House members to support any attempt to remove the Speaker
Limits when the Speaker can bypass normal House procedures to only Mondays, Tuesdays, and Wednesdays
Stops the House from considering bills that the budget office says would hurt long-term finances
Lets House committees vote by computer instead of only in person
Allows non-government witnesses to testify remotely in committee hearings
Shuts down the House Office of Diversity and Inclusion
Takes away some union rights for House staff members

119-hres-53.one_liner: Allow House votes on a forest management bill and an immigration enforcement bill
provisions: Sets up House votes on H.R. 471, which speeds up forest management on federal and tribal lands to reduce fire risk
Sets up House votes on S. 5, which requires holding non-citizens charged with theft in the US
Allows both bills to be debated and voted on under special House rules
AI summary temporarily unavailable
119-hres-93.one_liner: Providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 27) to amend the Controlled Substances Act with respect to the scheduling of fentanyl-related substances, and for other purposes.

119-hres-122.one_liner: Let Congress cancel multiple government regulations with a single vote
provisions: Changes the law so Congress can bundle together votes to cancel multiple regulations instead of voting on each one separately
Applies to rules issued by federal agencies in the final months before a new president takes office
Makes it faster for Congress to overturn regulations they disagree with

119-hres-161.one_liner: Allow House votes on canceling two federal rules and setting the federal budget
provisions: Sets up a House vote on canceling energy efficiency standards for gas water heaters
Sets up a House vote on canceling EPA fees on methane emissions from oil and gas companies
Sets up a House vote on the federal budget for 2025 and spending plans through 2034

119-hres-177.one_liner: Set rules for House votes to cancel three federal agency regulations
provisions: Sets rules for the House to vote on canceling a Department of Energy rule about appliance labels and testing requirements
Sets rules for the House to vote on canceling an EPA rule limiting air pollution from rubber tire factories
Sets rules for the House to vote on canceling a rule protecting underwater historical sites from offshore drilling
Limits debate on each vote to one hour
Prevents lawmakers from raising procedural objections during the votes

119-hres-189.one_liner: Formally criticize Representative Al Green for his conduct
provisions: The House of Representatives officially criticizes Representative Al Green of Texas
Creates a permanent record of this criticism in congressional files
Does not remove him from office or change his voting power

119-hres-211.one_liner: Set House rules for voting on digital asset reporting, unemployment fraud, and government funding bills
provisions: Sets House rules for debating a bill to cancel an IRS rule about reporting digital asset sales
Sets House rules for debating a bill to extend time limits for prosecuting unemployment fraud from the pandemic
Sets House rules for debating a bill to fund the government through September 30, 2025

119-hres-242.one_liner: Allow the House to vote on three bills about energy rules and foreign influence in universities
provisions: Sets up a House vote on canceling energy efficiency rules for walk-in coolers and freezers
Sets up a House vote on canceling energy efficiency rules for commercial refrigerators and freezers
Sets up a House vote on requiring universities to disclose foreign gifts and limiting contracts with certain countries

119-hres-282.one_liner: Set up House votes on bank overdraft rules, digital payment rules, court powers, and voter registration
provisions: Sets up a House vote to cancel a rule limiting overdraft fees charged by the biggest banks
Sets up a House vote to cancel a rule covering digital payment apps like Venmo and Cash App
Sets up a House vote on limiting when federal judges can block government actions with court orders
Sets up a House vote requiring people to show proof of US citizenship when registering to vote in federal elections

119-hres-294.one_liner: Set rules for House votes on four bills covering bank fees, court powers, and voting requirements
provisions: Allows the House to vote on canceling two bank regulation rules from the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
Allows the House to vote on limiting when federal courts can block government actions
Allows the House to vote on requiring proof of citizenship to register for federal elections
Sets the procedures and time limits for debating these four bills

119-hres-313.one_liner: Set rules for House vote on Senate changes to budget plan for 2025-2034
provisions: Lets the House vote on a Senate-changed version of the budget plan for the year starting October 2024 through 2034
Allows one hour of debate split equally between the Budget Committee chair and top Democrat
Blocks members from raising procedural objections during the vote
Skips the normal step of reading the Senate changes and the motion out loud

119-hres-352.one_liner: Ask officials and community leaders to condemn antisemitism
provisions: Asks elected officials to condemn acts of antisemitism
Asks faith leaders to condemn acts of antisemitism
Asks civil society leaders to condemn acts of antisemitism
Asks leaders to counter acts of antisemitism
Asks for public education about Jewish American community contributions

119-hres-354.one_liner: Schedule House votes on overturning five federal environmental and park rules
provisions: Schedules a House vote to overturn a National Park Service rule about motor vehicles in Glen Canyon
Schedules a House vote to overturn federal protection for an endangered fish in San Francisco Bay
Schedules three House votes to overturn EPA rules about California vehicle pollution standards
Sets the debate rules and voting procedures for all five measures

119-hres-377.one_liner: Allow House votes on renaming the Gulf of Mexico and restricting college funding
provisions: Sets up House votes on two separate bills
First bill renames the Gulf of Mexico as the Gulf of America
Second bill stops Department of Homeland Security money from going to colleges that work with Chinese government education programs
Creates rules for how long lawmakers can debate these bills
Decides which changes lawmakers can make to the bills before voting

119-hres-405.one_liner: Sets up House votes on three bills about police officer safety and weapons
provisions: Requires the Attorney General to write reports about violent attacks on police officers
Changes federal rules about when law enforcement officers can carry concealed weapons
Lets federal law enforcement officers buy their old service weapons when they retire

119-hres-426.one_liner: Set up House votes to cancel two federal rules on bank mergers and air pollution
provisions: Allows the House to vote on canceling an Office of the Comptroller of the Currency rule about reviewing bank merger applications
Allows the House to vote on canceling an EPA rule that reclassifies major air pollution sources as smaller area sources
Skips normal House procedural requirements to speed up these votes

119-hres-436.one_liner: Set rules for how the House will debate and vote on H.R. 1
provisions: Allows the House to consider H.R. 1 for a vote
Replaces the original H.R. 1 text with new text from Rules Committee Print 119-3
Sets 2 hours of debate time split equally between Budget Committee and Ways and Means Committee leaders
Allows one motion to send the bill back to committee before the final vote
Prevents lawmakers from raising certain procedural objections during debate

119-hres-458.one_liner: Allow House votes on four bills about opioid programs and small business rules
provisions: Lets the House debate and vote on renewing opioid treatment and prevention programs
Lets the House debate moving Small Business Administration offices out of sanctuary cities
Lets the House debate requiring citizenship papers for some small business loans
Lets the House debate putting a cap on how many small business lending companies can operate

119-hres-481.one_liner: Condemn attacks on Jewish people and state commitment to fight antisemitism
provisions: Condemns the June 1, 2025 attack in Boulder, Colorado as an act of terror
Condemns attacks on Jewish people across the United States
States the House will work to combat antisemitism
States the House will protect people's rights to gather peacefully and practice their religion without fear

119-hres-488.one_liner: Denounces an antisemitic terrorist attack in Boulder, Colorado
provisions: Formally condemns the antisemitic terrorist attack that occurred in Boulder, Colorado
Expresses sympathy for victims and their families
Reaffirms Congress opposition to antisemitism and terrorism

119-hres-489.one_liner: Set debate rules for four bills on DC voting, immigration, police, and fentanyl drug classification
provisions: Sets up House debate and voting procedures for four separate bills
Stops non-citizens from voting in DC elections and cancels a 2022 DC law that allowed non-citizen voting
Requires Washington DC to follow federal immigration laws
Lets DC police unions negotiate officer discipline rules through collective bargaining again
Changes how fentanyl-related drugs are classified under federal controlled substance laws
This resolution sets the rules for House debate on H.R. 4, the spending cuts bill.
It determines how long debate lasts, what changes are allowed, and the order of votes.
119-hres-499.one_liner: This resolution sets the rules for House debate on H.R. 4, the spending cuts bill.

119-hres-516.one_liner: Condemn June 2025 Los Angeles riots and thank law enforcement agencies
provisions: Says Congress condemns riots that happened in June 2025 in Los Angeles
Says Congress thanks law enforcement agencies for responding to the riots
Says Congress thanks U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement for their role

119-hres-519.one_liner: Condemn attacks on Minnesota lawmakers and call for rejection of political violence
provisions: Condemns the June 14, 2025 attacks on Minnesota state legislators in Brooklyn Park and Champlin
Calls on all Americans to reject political violence and support peaceful democracy
Honors Speaker Emerita Melissa Hortman and her husband Mark Hortman who were killed
Honors Senator John Hoffman and his wife Yvette Hoffman who were injured
Honors law enforcement officers who responded to the attacks

119-hres-530.one_liner: Set up House debate and voting rules for four bills
provisions: Allows the House to debate and vote on a bill funding military construction and VA programs for the year ending September 2026
Allows the House to debate and vote on a bill requiring monthly reports on special interest aliens trying to enter the US illegally
Allows the House to debate and vote on a bill making drunk driving convictions grounds to deport non-citizens
Allows the House to debate and vote on a resolution condemning June 2025 riots in Los Angeles

119-hres-537.one_liner: Impeach President Trump for taking Congress's power to declare war
provisions: Impeaches President Donald Trump from the presidency
Charges him with abuse of presidential powers
Says he took Congress's power to declare war for himself
Creates one article of impeachment against the president

119-hres-539.one_liner: Formally criticize Representative LaMonica McIver and remove her from the Committee on Homeland Security
provisions: Formally criticizes Representative LaMonica McIver through an official Congressional statement
Removes Representative McIver from the Committee on Homeland Security

119-hres-566.one_liner: Set rules for a House vote on Senate changes to budget bill H.R. 1
provisions: Allows the House to vote on whether to accept Senate changes to budget bill H.R. 1
Gives lawmakers 1 hour to debate the Senate changes
Splits debate time between Budget Committee leaders and Ways and Means Committee leaders
Prevents lawmakers from raising procedural objections during the vote
Requires a straight up-or-down vote without other motions

119-hres-580.one_liner: Set House rules for debating defense spending and financial regulation bills
provisions: Allows the House to debate and vote on the 2026 defense spending bill
Allows the House to debate and vote on a bill regulating digital currencies
Allows the House to debate and vote on a bill stopping the Federal Reserve from offering digital currency
Allows the House to debate and vote on a bill regulating stablecoins
Skips certain procedural requirements for considering these bills

119-hres-590.one_liner: Set up a vote on Senate changes to spending cuts proposed by the President
provisions: Sets up a vote on Senate changes to a House bill about government spending cuts
Covers government money the President wanted to cut on June 3, 2025
Uses rules from a 1974 law that controls how Congress handles the federal budget

119-hres-672.one_liner: Set up votes on energy funding and three land management rule cancellations
provisions: Sets up a vote on funding for energy and water programs for the year starting October 2025
Sets up votes to cancel three Bureau of Land Management rules about federal land use
The three rules govern grazing, mining, drilling, and recreation in Miles City Montana, North Dakota, and Central Yukon Alaska

119-s-146.one_liner: Ban posting intimate photos online without consent and require platforms to remove them
provisions: Makes it illegal to post intimate photos or videos online without the person's permission
Covers both real photos and computer-generated fake images
Creates prison time and fines for people who break this law
Requires lawbreakers to pay money to victims
Makes it illegal to threaten to post someone's intimate photos
Forces websites that allow user posts to remove reported content within 48 hours

119-s-1596.one_liner: Rename the Anahuac National Wildlife Refuge in Texas to Jocelyn Nungaray National Wildlife Refuge
provisions: Changes the name of the Anahuac National Wildlife Refuge in Texas to Jocelyn Nungaray National Wildlife Refuge

119-sconres-7.one_liner: Set the government's spending and income targets for the next 10 years
provisions: Sets the government's spending and income targets for each year from 2025 to 2034
Tells House and Senate committees to write laws that change the deficit by specific amounts
Requires committees to finish their work by March 7, 2025
Creates rules about how Congress can change spending during the budget process
Sets up special funds that let Congress adjust spending for certain types of laws

119-sres-195.one_liner: Request information on El Salvador's human rights practices
provisions: Asks the administration for information about human rights practices in El Salvador
Uses a 1961 law that lets Congress request human rights reports about countries that get US foreign aid
